activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis Essay
activities_projects.objective: Analyze complex themes, symbolism, and tone in Frost's poems.
steps: Select 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice'
Identify key symbols, tone, and mood
Write a structured essay analyzing the poet’s message and literary devices
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance critical thinking and literary analysis skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual support, and coherence

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Allegory Chart
activities_projects.objective: Examine deeper symbolic meanings and allegorical significance.
steps: Identify all symbols and imagery in both poems
Explain literal and figurative meanings of each symbol
Present findings in a detailed chart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand multi-layered literary devices.
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, accuracy, and depth of explanation

activities_projects.title: Poetic Perspective Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Analyze narrative voice and point of view across the poems.
steps: Identify the speaker’s perspective in each poem
Analyze how point of view influences interpretation
Write a comparative analysis of tone and perspective
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will refine understanding of narrative perspective in poetry.

activities_projects.title: Advanced Theme Synthesis
activities_projects.objective: Connect poem themes to philosophical or real-world contexts.
steps: Identify overarching themes like change, conflict, and morality
Relate these themes to contemporary life or personal experiences
Write a synthesis essay with examples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop higher-order thinking by linking literature to broader concepts.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Literary Devices Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how Frost uses literary devices differently in the two poems.
steps: List literary devices used in both poems (imagery, symbolism, alliteration, metaphor)
Explain how each device contributes to theme and meaning
Write a comparative paragraph highlighting differences and similarities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance analytical and interpretive skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, clarity, and depth of comparison

activities_projects.title: Creative Reinterpretation Project
activities_projects.objective: Reimagine a Frost poem in a modern context or personal perspective.
steps: Choose either 'Dust of Snow' or 'Fire and Ice'
Rewrite the poem in a modern context or personal experience
Include commentary on how the mood, symbols, or message change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will exercise creative thinking and literary adaptation skills.

activities_projects.title: Debate – Fire vs Ice
activities_projects.objective: Argue the destructive power of fire versus ice with textual support.
steps: Divide class into two groups: fire and ice
Each group presents arguments using examples and literary interpretation
Conclude with a reasoned judgment supported by text
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance debate skills, critical thinking, and textual interpretation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning, textual evidence, and persuasive ability

activities_projects.title: Emotion and Mood Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Perform an advanced study of emotional progression in the poems.
steps: Identify subtle shifts in tone and mood throughout the poems
Explain how imagery, rhythm, and word choice affect emotions
Create a detailed timeline or diagram showing mood progression
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will refine emotional and literary analysis skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth, accuracy, and clarity of diagram

activities_projects.title: Peer Teaching – Poetic Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Develop teaching and analytical skills by explaining poems to classmates.
steps: Choose a stanza or line from each poem
Prepare a mini-lesson explaining literary devices, mood, and theme
Present and guide peers in discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will reinforce understanding and communication skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, accuracy, and engagement of presentation

activities_projects.title: Research on Frost’s Philosophy
activities_projects.objective: Explore Frost’s worldview and recurring motifs across poems.
steps: Research Robert Frost’s philosophy and style
Identify recurring motifs and how they appear in 'Dust of Snow' and 'Fire and Ice'
Write a short report connecting philosophical ideas to the poems
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand literary philosophy and thematic continuity.
activities_projects.evaluation: Research accuracy, depth of insight, and clarity of writing
